title: Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients
summary: Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients retrieves prior skills in an original independent multi-theme color release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css color formats, css color relationships, css color contrast, css color not only, css gradients in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent multi-theme color release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey color and status work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the survey color, contrast, and decorative-layer system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: survey palette notation and alpha-compositing audit · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-theme color release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent multi-theme color release decision.

content.title: independent multi-theme color release decision evidence
content.text: For Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients, choose the css color formats claim that preserves the independent multi-theme color release decision purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Color, Contrast, Meaning, and Gradients checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-theme color release decision case 1: different color notations create inherently different displayed colors.

Independent multi-theme color release decision case 1: convert equivalent values, choose a format that makes the intended manipulation clear, and calculate or inspect alpha against the actual background stack.
Independent multi-theme color release decision case 1: use eight-digit hex whenever contrast is weak because alpha automatically darkens text.

options.text: Independent multi-theme color release decision case 2: a mathematically harmonious palette automatically communicates usable hierarchy.

Independent multi-theme color release decision case 2: assign colors to content and state roles, explore relationships, preserve contrast and non-color cues, and test meaning with representative contexts.
Independent multi-theme color release decision case 2: choose warm colors for danger in every culture and product without testing interpretation.

options.text: independent multi-theme color release decision phase 3.1: inventory foreground-background pairs for every content and interaction state
independent multi-theme color release decision phase 3.2: classify text size component graphic and focus requirements
independent multi-theme color release decision phase 3.3: measure rendered pairs including opacity images and gradient extremes
independent multi-theme color release decision phase 3.4: adjust roles and retest default hover focus active disabled error and theme states

options.text: Independent multi-theme color release decision case 4: adding a second shade is enough to avoid a color-only distinction.
Independent multi-theme color release decision case 4: pair every meaningful color difference with text, structure, shape, pattern, or programmatic state and verify the task without author colors.
Independent multi-theme color release decision case 4: add decorative icons without text or names and assume every user recognizes them.

options.text: Independent multi-theme color release decision case 5: a gradient is an image element and needs an alt attribute.

Independent multi-theme color release decision case 5: keep gradient information decorative, declare a solid fallback first, control stops and geometry, and test contrast across every painted location.
Independent multi-theme color release decision case 5: encode the only capacity legend in a conic gradient because its colors are self-explanatory.
